articles.title: Musk says Tesla’s FSD 14.3 set for broader release by week’s end (TSLA:NASDAQ)
articles.summary: Elon Musk announced that Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) version 14.3 is currently in employee beta testing. He anticipates a wider rollout of the software to the public by the end of the current week. Tesla (TSLA) shares are being closely watched following this development.

articles.title: NTSB Finds Ford's BlueCruise Driver Assistance System At Fault For Two Fatal Collisions
articles.summary: The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) has attributed two fatal collisions in early 2024 to Ford's BlueCruise driver assistance system, citing its failure to stop for stationary vehicles and detect driver disengagement. The NTSB is calling for stronger safety standards, better oversight, and improvements to crash data recording and driver monitoring systems for partial automation technologies. This comes as Ford plans to offer "eyes-off" technology by 2028 and amid an ongoing NHTSA investigation into Tesla's Full Self-Driving system.

This comes as Ford plans to offer "eyes-off" technology by 2028 and amid an ongoing NHTSA investigation into Tesla's Full Self-Driving system.

articles.title: Why UBER Stock At $70 Is A Steal
articles.summary: Uber Technologies (UBER) is presented as a compelling investment at $70, having transitioned from a high-burn startup to a profitable cash-flow engine. The company benefits from strong advertising revenue growth, massive user scale, a successful membership program (Uber One), and increasing free cash flow driven by operating leverage. However, the article also addresses significant risks, including potential regulatory changes impacting driver classification and the emerging threat of robotaxi services from competitors like Tesla and Waymo.

articles.title: TD (TD) offers $1,000 auto‑callable, contingent‑coupon notes linked to AVGO/META/NVDA/TSLA
articles.summary: The Toronto-Dominion Bank is offering $1,000 equity-linked, auto-callable notes with contingent coupons tied to the performance of Broadcom (AVGO), Meta (META), NVIDIA (NVDA), and Tesla (TSLA). These notes feature a minimum 24.85% annual contingent coupon rate, an April 10, 2026 pricing date, an April 15, 2026 issue date, and an April 13, 2029 maturity date. Payments and automatic calls are contingent on the lowest performing underlying stock, exposing investors to significant downside risk if any one of the stocks performs poorly.

articles.title: Tesla French car registrations triple in March
articles.summary: Tesla's new car registrations in March more than tripled in France and doubled in Denmark, signaling a recovery in the EV giant's sales in Europe. After a difficult year marred by increasing competition and lack of new models, the introduction of cheaper Model Y and Model 3 versions has led to a return to growth. Registrations in France reached 9,569 units, a 203.10% increase year-over-year, while Denmark saw a 144% rise to 1,447 units.

articles.title: GraniteShares Announces Weekly Distributions for its Autocallable ETFs: ANV and TLA
articles.summary: GraniteShares announced the weekly distributions for its Autocallable ETFs, ANV (GraniteShares Autocallable NVDA ETF) and TLA (GraniteShares Autocallable TSLA ETF). The announcement details the distribution per share, distribution rate, 30-day SEC yield, ex-date, record date, and payment date for both ETFs. The company also provides important disclaimers regarding distribution guarantees, performance, and investment risks associated with these funds.

articles.title: Tesla (TSLA) closes above ___ on April 1? Trading Odds & Predictions
articles.summary: Polymarket hosts a prediction market for Tesla (TSLA) stock's closing price on April 1, 2026, with several price thresholds available for trading. Tesla shares are currently trading around $381.59, influenced by optimism over Q1 delivery figures and a new FSD software launch. The market resolution depends on Yahoo Finance's official closing price, with rules in place for shortened sessions, trading halts, and stock splits.

articles.title: Tesla (TESLA) and SpaceX Plan to Build a New Chip Factory in Texas
articles.summary: Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) and SpaceX are planning to build two advanced chip factories in Austin, Texas, as part of a "Terafab" project. This initiative aims to address future chip demand for Tesla's vehicles and Optimus robots, as well as AI data centers in space, given that current global supply only meets a small fraction of their projected needs. The Terafab venture is a collaboration involving SpaceX, Tesla, and xAI, with no completion date announced.

articles.title: Tesla named on Iran’s 18-company target list threatening Gulf operations
articles.summary: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has included Tesla on a list of 18 US companies whose Middle East operations are threatened with attack in retaliation for military leader killings, setting an explicit deadline of April 1. Tesla's significant physical presence in the Gulf, including showrooms and Supercharger stations, makes it a unique target compared to other tech and defense companies primarily operating data centers. The threat comes as Tesla has been actively managing regional instability, including offering free Supercharging, and its inclusion on the list is notable given its lack of defense contracts.

articles.title: Tradr 2X Short TSLA Daily ETF
articles.summary: This page provides a detailed profile of the Tradr 2X Short TSLA Daily ETF (TSLQ), an inverse equity ETF designed to track the inverse performance of Tesla Inc. It includes current pricing, expense ratios, holdings, performance data, technical analysis, and comparisons to other inverse equity ETFs. The ETF aims to provide 2x short exposure to TSLA's daily returns.
